question.text: The Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957 were made by the Central Government in exercise of powers conferred by which section of SCRA?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SCRR was made in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 30 of the SCRA. **The Trap:** Section 23 deals with penalties, not rule-making powers, and may mislead candidates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding the source of delegated legislation is important across all NISM certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 30 is the rule-making power of the Central Government under SCRA, similar to section-level delegations in other Acts.
